content:
First write \(r=[r]+ \{r\}=n+f\) with \(n\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge0}\), \(0\le f<1\).  The equation is
\[
25f+n=125
\;\Longrightarrow\;
f=\frac{125-n}{25}=5-\frac n{25}.
\]
We need \(0\le f<1\), i.e.
\[
0\le5-\frac n{25}<1
\;\Longrightarrow\;
0\le5-\tfrac n{25}
\quad\text{and}\quad
5-\tfrac n{25}<1,
\]
so
\[
n\le125
\quad\text{and}\quad
n>100.
\]
Thus \(n=101,102,\dots,125\) (25 values).  For each such \(n\),
\[
r=n+f=n+5-\frac n{25}=5+\frac{24}{25}n.
\]
Hence
\[
\sum r
=\sum_{n=101}^{125}\Bigl(5+\frac{24}{25}n\Bigr)
=25\cdot5+\frac{24}{25}\sum_{n=101}^{125}n
=125+\frac{24}{25}\cdot2825
=125+24\cdot113
=125+2712
=2837.
\]
Answer: \(\boxed{2837}\).

\[

\;\Longrightarrow\;
f=\frac{125-n}{25}=5-\frac n{25}.
\]
We need \(0\le f<1\), i.e.
\[
0\le5-\frac n{25}<1
\;\Longrightarrow\;
0\le5-\tfrac n{25}
\quad\text{and}\quad
5-\tfrac n{25}<1,
\]
so
\[
n\le125
\quad\text{and}\quad
n>100.
\]
Thus \(n=101,102,\dots,125\) (25 values).  For each such \(n\),
\[
r=n+f=n+5-\frac n{25}=5+\frac{24}{25}n.

\[

=25\cdot5+\frac{24}{25}\sum_{n=101}^{125}n

=125+24\cdot113
=125+2712
=2837.
\]
Answer: \(\boxed{2837}\).
